name: Primes
javadoc:
/** * Methods related to prime numbers in the range of int: *
    *
  • primality test
    • *
  • prime number generation
    • *
  • factorization
    • *
* * @since 3.2 */

members.name: isPrime
members.javadoc:
/** * Primality test: tells if the argument is a (provable) prime or not. *

* It uses the Miller-Rabin probabilistic test in such a way that a result is guaranteed: * it uses the firsts prime numbers as successive base (see Handbook of applied cryptography * by Menezes, table 4.1). * * @param n number to test. * @return true if n is prime. (All numbers < 2 return false). */

tags.name: n
tags.description: n must be a positive integer

tags.content: true if n is prime. (All numbers < 2 return false).
tags.description: true if n is prime. (All numbers < 2 return false).

members.name: nextPrime
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Return the smallest prime greater than or equal to n.
 *
 * @param n a positive number.
 * @return the smallest prime greater than or equal to n.
 * @throws MathIllegalArgumentException if n < 0.
 */

tags.name: n
tags.description: n must be a positive number

tags.content: the smallest prime greater than or equal to n.
tags.description: the smallest prime greater than or equal to n.

members.name: primeFactors
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Prime factors decomposition
 *
 * @param n number to factorize: must be ≥ 2
 * @return list of prime factors of n
 * @throws MathIllegalArgumentException if n < 2.
 */

tags.content: list of prime factors of n
